preamble: The voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] (Arabic "ain") is often lost in languages without pharyngeals.

examples.note: In modern Abkhaz (contrasting with conservative Abaza), historical pharyngeal /ʕ/ has been deleted, though it conditions vowel length (e.g., /aː/ as residue of /ʕa/).
Modern Hebrew treats it as zero or glottal stop.

sources: Gesenius, W. & Kautzsch, E. (1910). Gesenius' Hebrew Grammar. https://archive.org/details/geseniushebrewgr00geseuoft
